item.title: His father had just been buried. Then West Bank settlers forced him to dig up the body
item.description: The UN human rights office condemns incident as "'appalling and emblematic of the dehumanisation of Palestinians" in the West Bank.

item.title: Oil prices jump after Trump dismisses Iran proposal to end war
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz waterway remains effectively shut, severely disrupting global energy shipments.

item.title: TikTok scales back AI-generated video descriptions after absurd errors
item.description: While only rolled out to some users, the feature's bizarre AI-generated descriptions were shared widely.

item.title: British Steel nationalisation plans announced by Starmer
item.description: The steelworks has been under government control for almost a year, but leglistation to nationalise it will be put forward this week.

item.title: Instagram privacy tech is turned off today - what does this mean for your DMs?
item.description: The platform said it would remove end-to-end encrypted messages, a major U‑turn by parent company Meta.

item.title: Massive Alaska megatsunami was second largest ever recorded
item.description: New research suggests glacier melt driven by climate change is increasing the risk of giant waves.

item.title: International cyber attack disrupts swathe of universities and schools
item.description: A hacking group breached the academic software Canvas, used by thousands of schools and universities across the globe.

item.title: This couple lost £1,000 after their flight was cancelled - here is what to check so you don't
item.description: It's essential to read your policy details carefully - here's a checklist to tick off before you set off on your travels.

item.title: TikTok launches £3.99 subscription for no ads in UK
item.description: Those who don't subscribe will be able to use the platform for free - but be shown personalised ads by default.

item.title: Ovo energy customers urged not to panic as takeover planned
item.description: All existing tariffs will be honoured in full under a planned deal that could create Britain's largest energy supplier.

item.title: Portrait looted by Nazis found in home of Dutch SS leader's descendants
item.description: The painting is believed to have been plundered by high-ranking Nazi Hermann Goering during World War Two.

item.title: Is this actually what Anne Boleyn looked like?
item.description: An algorithm has suggested images of what were thought to be Anne Boleyn might not be.

item.title: Is this the real face of Anne Boleyn?
item.description: A computer science team believes they have discovered a previously unknown sketch of King Henry VIII's second wife - but not everyone is convinced.

item.title: TikTok launches £3.99 subscription for no ads in UK
item.description: Those who don't subscribe will be able to use the platform for free - but be shown personalised ads by default.

item.title: Trump calls Iran response to US proposal to end war 'totally unacceptable'
item.description: Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage.

item.title: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
item.description: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

item.title: Ethiopian woman's joy at rare quintuplets after 12 years trying for a baby
item.description: The woman, 35, says she was praying for a baby and was "overjoyed" to be "blessed with five at once".

item.title: French national shows symptoms on return from hantavirus-hit ship
item.description: Five passengers of the MV Hondius will be quarantined in Paris "until further notice", France's prime minister says.

item.title: Philippine VP Sara Duterte impeached for a second time
item.description: If convicted in a Senate trial, which is uncertain, she will be disqualified from running for president.

item.title: US and French nationals test positive for hantavirus after leaving ship
item.description: The American national has arrived in Nebraska, while a French woman is isolating in Paris.

item.title: What is hantavirus and how does it spread?
item.description: The Andes strain of the virus, which can rarely be passed from person to person, has been found among passengers of a cruise ship.

item.title: How sunburn inspired a new way to store energy
item.description: Molecules that can capture heat could be a useful technology to decarbonise heating.

item.title: £20m mystery gift buys London Zoo new hospital where you can watch vets work
item.description: Visitors will be able to watch live veterinary procedures inside a state-of-the-art new animal hospital.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship on way to Canary Islands after three evacuated
item.description: A British man is among three evacuees sent to the Netherlands after displaying symptoms while aboard the MV Hondius.

item.title: How worried should we be about hantavirus?
item.description: Passengers potentially exposed to hantavirus are being repatriated, so what is the risk to the wider public?
